subject: Geology
title: What Is Metamorphism?
description: A quick win: understand What Is Metamorphism?.

hook.text: Squeeze limestone hard enough and long enough, and it becomes marble. Heat and pressure transform rock.

buildup.text: Metamorphism is the process of changing existing rock through heat, pressure, or chemical fluids without melting.

discovery.text: The key word is 'without melting.' If the rock melts, it becomes magma, and any new rock would be igneous.

twist.text: Metamorphism rearranges atoms and grows new minerals. The original rock's chemistry stays, but its structure changes.

climax.text: Metamorphic rocks reveal the hidden forces deep underground: collisions, burial, and extreme conditions.

punchline.text: Pressure turns ordinary rock into something new.

quiz.question: What makes metamorphism different from melting?
options: It only happens at the surface
